title: Rate-Distortion Theory: Lossy Compression
description: Master rate-distortion theory - the fundamental theory of lossy data compression that establishes the minimum rate needed to represent a source within a given distortion.
learningObjectives: Define the rate-distortion function and its operational meaning
Calculate rate-distortion for binary and Gaussian sources
Understand the relationship between rate, distortion, and source entropy
Analyze distortion measures including Hamming and squared error
Connect rate-distortion to practical compression applications
